chunks.text: Prabal Dutta is a Professor at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office location at 550C Cory Hall and email prabal@berkeley.edu. He maintains a personal homepage and has publications and dissertations available.
chunks.question: What is Prabal Dutta's position and contact details?

chunks.text: Prabal Dutta earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 2009. He completed an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from The Ohio State University in 2004 and a B.S. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from The Ohio State University in 1997.
chunks.question: What are Prabal Dutta's educational qualifications?

chunks.text: Prabal Dutta's research areas include Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), Cyber-Physical Systems and Design Automation (CPSDA), Power and Energy (ENE), and Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT). He is affiliated with research centers CITRIS and iCyPhy.
chunks.question: What are Prabal Dutta's main research areas?

chunks.text: Prabal Dutta teaches courses including CS 294-194 Research to Startup on Thursdays 17:00-18:29 in Soda 310 and EECS 16B Introduction to Circuits & Devices on Tuesdays and Thursdays 14:00-15:29 in Valley Life Sciences 2050.
chunks.question: What courses does Prabal Dutta teach?

chunks.text: Prabal Dutta has received several honors and awards, including the Ohio State University COE Distinguished Alumni Award in 2025, Okawa Research Grant in 2022, ACM SENSYS Test of Time Award in 2022, Sloan Research Fellow in 2017, NSF CAREER Award in 2014, and Brilliant 10 in 2014.
chunks.question: What awards has Prabal Dutta received?
chunks.answer: Distinguished Alumni and Sloan Research Fellow.
